description: Build the book-craft pipeline end-to-end: source publisher samples, ingest open + sample books into llm-wiki-book-craft vault, build a /rubric skill that scores generated artifacts, run a closed-loop test case on a small topic, then update generate-book based on what the rubric reveals. Phase 1 (open-book sourcing) is already complete; this PRD covers Phases 2-6.

userStories.title: Phase 2 — Fetch For Dummies samples + cheat sheets
userStories.description: As the book-craft vault, I want voice exemplars from the For Dummies series so I can tune the dummies template-voice contract beyond what bundle-hints.md captures.
acceptanceCriteria: Fetch sample chapters / Look-Inside PDFs for 3 dev-relevant For Dummies titles. Suggested: JavaScript All-in-One For Dummies, Python All-in-One For Dummies, AWS For Dummies. Substitute if a title's free preview is unavailable.
Fetch 3 free Cheat Sheets from dummies.com (Wiley publishes these openly).
Land everything under vaults/llm-wiki-book-craft/raw/samples/dummies/ as PDFs or HTML, named by title slug.
Use WebFetch / curl. Do NOT pirate full books from third-party uploaders.
Append a [phase-2 dummies] entry to vaults/llm-wiki-book-craft/log.md with provenance URLs.
Commit (vault repo) with message: 📝 docs(samples): fetch For Dummies voice exemplars
Push if remote configured; otherwise log 'no remote' and continue.
userStories.notes: Completed 2026-04-26. Fetched 3 cheat sheets to raw/samples/dummies/. Sample chapters skipped (locked behind interactive viewers); cheat sheets carry the voice signal needed.

userStories.title: Phase 4 — Scaffold /rubric skill
userStories.description: As the engine, I want a /rubric skill that defines per-artifact-type quality rubrics so verify-quick.sh, /verify-artifact, and /ralph all read from a single source of truth instead of inlining their own checks.
acceptanceCriteria: Create .claude/skills/rubric/SKILL.md with frontmatter (name: rubric, description: aggressive — 'PREFER over inline quality checks', argument-hint: '<subcommand> [args]', allowed-tools includes Bash + Read + Write + Edit).
Subcommands: `list` (show rubrics defined for each type), `audit <type> <artifact>` (score one artifact), `report <type> <artifact>` (markdown report).
Create .claude/skills/rubric/types/ directory (empty for now; book.yaml lands in US-402).
Create .claude/skills/rubric/lib/ directory for the scoring engine (lands in US-403).
SKILL.md documents the rubric YAML schema: dimensions list, each with name, weight, gates (pass/warn thresholds), how-measured.

Cross-link from .claude/skills/generate/lib/quality-rubric.md (existing): note that /rubric is the new authoritative form; quality-rubric.md is the prose version, /rubric is the executable one. Both stay in sync.
Commit (engine repo): ✨ feat(rubric): scaffold /rubric skill
Push to engine repo origin if configured.

userStories.title: Phase 4 — Define types/book.yaml dimensions
userStories.description: As the rubric skill, I want a comprehensive book rubric YAML capturing every dimension a generated book is judged on so verify-quick can score it.
acceptanceCriteria: Create .claude/skills/rubric/types/book.yaml.
Top-level keys: type (book), version (1), description, dimensions (list).
Required dimensions (8 minimum, each with name + weight + gates + how-measured):
1. scope-fidelity — does the book cover only the topic, no off-topic drift? Measured: per-chapter title vs requested topic Jaccard.
2. depth — meets word + chapter floor (book: 3000 words, 3 chapters minimum). Measured: bundle.md word count + H1 count.
3. voice-fit — matches selected template's bundle-hints + (when present) user voice profile. Measured: presence of template signature phrases, absence of refusals.
4. template-fit — structural elements present (chapter openers, callouts, cheat sheets per template). Measured: regex on bundle.md.
5. layout — typography + margins + page breaks behaving (no widows/orphans, no overflow, page breaks where chapters start). Measured: PDF render check via pdftotext + page boundary inspection.
6. cover — cover page present, image-quality threshold (>500KB PNG, expected aspect ratio). Measured: file existence + size + dimensions.

7. ai-slop — absence of em-dashes, AI-tells (delve, leverage, robust, tapestry, etc.), rhetorical-reversal filler. Measured: regex blacklist scan on bundle.md. THIS IS A HARD GATE — any hit is a fail.
8. source-coverage — every paragraph traceable to a source page in generated-from. Measured: sample N paragraphs, attempt to locate substring in source pages, expect ≥80% hits.
Each dimension's gates: { pass: <numeric or boolean>, warn: <looser threshold> }. Score: pass / warn / fail.
Overall verdict: ALL pass = pass. Any hard-gate fail = fail. Otherwise warn.
Commit: ✨ feat(rubric): define book.yaml with 8 dimensions
Push if remote configured.

userStories.title: Phase 4 — Implement rubric scoring engine
userStories.description: As the /rubric skill, I want a scoring engine that reads types/<type>.yaml + an artifact path and emits per-dimension scores + an overall verdict.
acceptanceCriteria: Create .claude/skills/rubric/lib/score-rubric.sh — takes <type> <artifact-path> <sidecar-path>, reads types/<type>.yaml, runs each dimension's how-measured, emits JSON: { dimensions: [{name, score, value, threshold}], overall: pass|warn|fail, hard_failures: [], warnings: [] }.
Use Python embedded in the bash script (heredoc) for YAML parsing + regex measurement, since the existing verify-quick.sh uses the same pattern.
Each how-measured implementation must be a small Python function — keep them short, all in one file.
Patches the artifact's sidecar with: rubric: { version, type, scored-at, dimensions: [...], overall: pass|warn|fail, hard_failures: [...], warnings: [...] }
Exit code: 0 = pass, 1 = warn, 2 = fail (hard gate hit).
Test: run against vaults/llm-wiki-research/artifacts/book/golden-stack-2026-04-26.pdf — should produce a structured score report. Note any false positives in the progress log.
Commit: ✨ feat(rubric): implement score-rubric.sh engine
Push if remote configured.

userStories.title: Phase 4 — Wire verify-quick.sh to read rubric/types
userStories.description: As verify-quick.sh, I want to delegate to /rubric instead of inlining its own checks so the depth + engagement + sidecar checks stay aligned with the canonical rubric.
acceptanceCriteria: Edit .claude/skills/generate/lib/verify-quick.sh.
If .claude/skills/rubric/types/<type>.yaml exists: invoke .claude/skills/rubric/lib/score-rubric.sh <type> <artifact> <sidecar> and use its result.
If not: fall back to existing inline checks (backwards compatibility).
Stamp the sidecar with: quality.verify-quick = pass|warn|fail derived from rubric overall verdict.
Print a clear status summary: ✅ pass (N dimensions) / ⚠️ warn (N pass, M warn) / ❌ fail (hard gate: <name>).
Re-run on the Golden Stack book. Output must match what /rubric audit produces directly.
Commit: ♻️ refactor(verify-quick): delegate to /rubric for type-typed checks
Push if remote configured.

userStories.title: Phase 4 — Add /rubric audit subcommand + portal integration
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to run `/rubric audit <type> <artifact>` directly to score any pre-existing artifact, plus have rubric scores show up in the artifact portal.
acceptanceCriteria: Implement the audit subcommand handler inside .claude/skills/rubric/SKILL.md (Step N) — parses args, calls score-rubric.sh, prints the human report.
Implement the report subcommand — same scoring but emits a markdown report file alongside the artifact (artifact.rubric-report.md).
If .claude/skills/generate-portal/ exists: update its renderer to show rubric.overall + per-dimension chips on each card. If not, just note in progress.txt.
Re-render the engine repo's docs page that catalogues skills (sites/docs/src/content/docs/features/) to include /rubric. (Light touch — one new page.)
Commit: ✨ feat(rubric): add audit + report subcommands and portal integration
Push if remote configured.

userStories.title: Phase 5 — Generate qmd-architecture book in 4 themes (sample mode)
userStories.description: As the rubric closed-loop test, I want to generate the qmd-architecture topic as a sample book (chapter 1 only) in all 4 themes, score each via the rubric, and identify the worst performer.
acceptanceCriteria: Test topic: vaults/llm-wiki-ai-research/wiki/concepts/qmd-architecture.md (a single page, ~750 lines, fits a chapter).
Run /generate book qmd-architecture --vault llm-wiki-ai-research --theme observatory --sample (chapter 1 only).
Repeat for --theme dummies, --theme oreilly, --theme headfirst.
All 4 PDFs land in vaults/llm-wiki-ai-research/artifacts/book/samples/.
Run /rubric audit book <each-pdf> --sidecar <each.meta.yaml>. All 4 rubric reports stored.
Produce a comparison table in vaults/llm-wiki-book-craft/wiki/comparisons/rubric-test-qmd-architecture-2026-04-26.md showing per-dimension scores across 4 themes + the worst-performing theme.
Commit (across both vaults + engine if needed): 🧪 test(rubric): score qmd-architecture sample books across 4 themes
Push as available.

userStories.title: Phase 5 — Iterate worst theme
userStories.description: As the rubric closed-loop test, I want to take the worst-scoring theme from US-501, identify the specific dimensions that failed, regenerate with rubric feedback applied, and re-score.
acceptanceCriteria: Read vaults/llm-wiki-book-craft/wiki/comparisons/rubric-test-qmd-architecture-2026-04-26.md to identify the worst theme + which dimensions tripped.

Apply targeted fix: edit themes/<worst>/bundle-hints.md, or themes/<worst>/theme.css, or generate-book/SKILL.md to address the specific failure mode (e.g. cover too small, AI-slop hits, missing cheat sheets, scope drift).
Re-run /generate book qmd-architecture --theme <worst> --sample.
Re-score with /rubric audit. Append the new score to the comparison page with a note on what changed and the delta.
If Nano Banana cover scored low: re-invoke ro:generate-image with --count 4 and pick the best.
Commit: ✨ feat(themes/<worst>): apply rubric-driven fixes; <delta> score improvement
Push as available.

userStories.title: Phase 5 — Re-iterate or conclude
userStories.description: As the rubric closed-loop test, I want to keep iterating on the worst theme until either it plateaus or it passes.
acceptanceCriteria: Read the latest rubric scores for the iterated theme.
If overall verdict moved from fail → warn → pass: stop, success.
If improvement < 5% across all dimensions over the last iteration: stop, plateau.
Otherwise: apply ONE more targeted fix (same shape as US-502) and re-score.
Maximum 2 additional iterations within this story; if still failing, raise the issue in progress.txt and stop.
Update the comparison page with the final iteration log.
Commit: 🧪 test(rubric): close the loop on qmd-architecture iteration
Push as available.

userStories.title: Phase 6 — Roll lessons into themes/*/bundle-hints.md
userStories.description: As the engine, I want every theme's bundle-hints.md updated based on what Phase 3 syntheses + Phase 5 iterations revealed so future /generate book runs benefit from what we learned.
acceptanceCriteria: Read each wiki/concepts/template-voice-*.md page's 'Recommended bundle-hints additions' section.
Edit .claude/skills/generate-book/themes/{observatory,dummies,oreilly,headfirst}/bundle-hints.md to incorporate the recommendations. Keep the existing structure; add to it.

If template-voice-inaction.md is solid: scaffold themes/inaction/ with bundle-hints.md, theme.css (start as a fork of oreilly's CSS, tuned for In-Action's hands-on feel), and cover-prompt.md. Otherwise note as deferred.
Re-run /rubric audit on the Phase 5 iteration's final PDFs to confirm scores hold.
Commit: ✨ feat(themes): roll Phase 3-5 learnings into bundle-hints.md
Push as available.

userStories.title: Phase 6 — Re-render Golden Stack book v2 + score delta
userStories.description: As proof the rubric loop pays off, I want to re-render the Golden Stack book (the original Dummies one) with the updated themes/dummies/bundle-hints and compare rubric scores v1 vs v2.
acceptanceCriteria: Run /generate book "golden stack" --vault llm-wiki-research --theme dummies. Note: pages don't auto-resolve from the topic string; pass the canonical 3 docs explicitly via the bundle pre-step or use a folder/tag the user has tagged. If select-pages.sh still doesn't match, document the workaround in the progress log.
New artifact lands at vaults/llm-wiki-research/artifacts/book/golden-stack-2026-04-26-v2.pdf with sidecar version: 2 and replaces: golden-stack-2026-04-26.pdf.
Run /rubric audit book on both v1 and v2; produce a delta report.
Land the delta report at vaults/llm-wiki-book-craft/wiki/comparisons/rubric-delta-golden-stack-v1-v2-2026-04-26.md.
If v2 scores worse than v1: investigate, fix the regression, regenerate. Don't ship a worse v2.
Open both PDFs in the default viewer for visual confirmation: open <v1>; open <v2>.
Commit: 🧪 test(rubric): Golden Stack book v2 — score delta validates the loop
Push as available.
